title: Marry Your Like: Assortative Mating and Income Inequality
authors: Jeremy Greenwood, Nezih Guner, Georgi Kocharkov, Cezar Santos
survey: U.S. Census / American Community Survey (ACS) micro-data, married couples, years 1960, 1970, 1980, 1990, 2000, 2005
statistic: positive assortative mating on EDUCATION (five levels: <HS, HS, some college, college, post-college). Two summary measures: Kendall's tau rank correlation between a husband's and wife's education; and delta_t, the relative sum of the contingency-table diagonals (observed diagonal mass / independence-expected diagonal mass).

provenance.source: Greenwood, Jeremy, Nezih Guner, Georgi Kocharkov, and Cezar Santos. 2014. 'Marry Your Like: Assortative Mating and Income Inequality.' American Economic Review: Papers & Proceedings 104(5): 348-353.
provenance.figure: Figure 1 ('The Rise in Positive Assortative Mating, 1960-2005'): Kendall's tau (right panel) and delta_t, relative sum of diagonals (right panel, second axis).
provenance.fetch_method: HTTPS GET of the NBER working-paper PDF on 2026-07-10; text extracted with pdftotext. The tau and delta_t series are published as a FIGURE, not a value table, so the endpoint values are read from the figure axes (a figure-read range, recorded honestly as such), not transcribed from a numeric table.
provenance.verbatim_quote: Kendall's [tau] rank correlation is computed between a husband's and wife's education for each year. The changes in Kendall's [tau] over time are illustrated in Figure 1 ... Kendall's [tau] is clearly higher in 2005 relative to 1960.
provenance.note: EDUCATIONAL assortative mating, a proxy for the earnings-capacity sorting the gate-2c axis measures directly; the bridge therefore carries an education-vs-earnings concept delta on top of the rank-construct delta. The paper also reports the married-couple income Gini rising from 0.34 (1960) to 0.43 (2005), and that imposing random matching in 2005 would drop the Gini to 0.34.

education_relative_sum_of_diagonals_delt.definition: observed diagonal contingency mass divided by the independence-expected diagonal mass; delta_t > 1 means positive sorting

concept_note: delta_t (relative sum of contingency diagonals) is directly comparable to the same statistic computed on the gate-2c own-tercile x spouse-tercile earnings contingency, modulo (a) 5x5 education levels vs 3x3 earnings terciles and (b) education vs earnings-capacity. Reported for DIRECTION and order of magnitude only; no gate-2c value is calibrated to it.
